Amanita phalloides.toxicity_detail: Causes liver and kidney failure. Lethal dose is a single cap. Symptoms delayed 6-12 hours.
Amanita phalloides.symptoms: Delayed onset (6-12h): severe GI distress, apparent recovery, then liver failure on day 3-5
Amanita phalloides.first_aid: Immediately go to hospital. Mention possible amatoxin ingestion. Do NOT induce vomiting without medical guidance.

lookalikes.distinguishing: Death cap has white gills (not pink/brown), volva at base, greenish cap

Amanita phalloides.key_features: Green to yellow cap, white gills, skirt-like ring, cup-like volva at base
Amanita phalloides.survival_note: One of the world's most dangerous mushrooms. Responsible for 90% of mushroom poisoning deaths worldwide.

Amanita virosa.common_name_ja: シロタマゴテングタケ
Amanita virosa.toxicity_detail: Contains deadly amatoxins. Even small amounts can be fatal within days.
Amanita virosa.symptoms: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, liver failure, death within 2-7 days
Amanita virosa.first_aid: EMERGENCY HOSPITAL. This is life-threatening. Do not induce vomiting.
Amanita virosa.key_features: Pure white cap and stem, ring, volva at base, white gills
Amanita virosa.survival_note: Completely white Amanita. Every part is deadly. Never consume.

Galerina marginata.toxicity_detail: Contains orellanine which causes kidney failure. Lethal within days.
Galerina marginata.symptoms: Thirst, nausea, vomiting, kidney pain, renal failure
Galerina marginata.first_aid: IMMEDIATE MEDICAL CARE. Kidney failure can be fatal. Do not induce vomiting.

lookalikes.distinguishing: Galerina has rusty-brown spore print, grows on wood, smaller cap

Galerina marginata.key_features: Small brown cap with lighter margin, rusty brown gills, grows on conifer wood
Galerina marginata.survival_note: Small brown mushroom that can be confused with edible species. Deadly.

Aconitum napellus.toxicity_detail: One of most toxic plants in Europe. Lethal dose is as low as 2-4 mg of aconitine.
Aconitum napellus.symptoms: Burning sensation in mouth, vomiting, diarrhea, muscle weakness, cardiac arrest
Aconitum napellus.first_aid: CALL EMERGENCY IMMEDIATELY. This is a life-threatening poisoning. Do not induce vomiting.

lookalikes.distinguishing: Delphinium has spurred flowers, Aconitum has helmet-shaped flowers

Aconitum napellus.key_features: Dark blue to purple helmet-shaped flowers in dense spikes
Aconitum napellus.survival_note: Lethal poison. Never consume any part of this plant.

Cicuta virosa.toxicity_detail: Cicutoxin is a violent convulsant poison. Death can occur within 15 minutes to 6 hours.
Cicuta virosa.symptoms: Nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, seizures, respiratory failure
Cicuta virosa.first_aid: EMERGENCY IMMEDIATE. This poison can kill in minutes. Call 911 immediately.

lookalikes.distinguishing: Cicuta has hollow chambered rhizome with septa; Oenanthe has solid rhizome and distinct aroma

Cicuta virosa.key_features: White umbelliferous flowers, hollow stem with purple spots, hollow chambered rhizome
Cicuta virosa.survival_note: Japan's three most toxic plants. Can be confused with edible water dropwort.

Veratrum album.toxicity_detail: Contains potent alkaloids that affect heart and nervous system.
Veratrum album.symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, cardiac arrhythmia, respiratory depression
Veratrum album.first_aid: IMMEDIATE MEDICAL ATTENTION. Do not induce vomiting.

lookalikes.common_name: Victory Onion / ギョウジャニンニク
lookalikes.distinguishing: Allium has onion/garlic odor; Veratrum has no smell and distinct leaf arrangement

Veratrum album.key_features: Tall plant with large leaves and greenish-white flowers in dense clusters
Veratrum album.survival_note: Toxic. Can be confused with wild onion. No onion smell is key difference.

lookalikes.distinguishing: Chanterelle has ridges instead of gills, false gills that fork; Jack-o'-lantern has true gills

Cantharellus cibarius.key_features: Golden to yellow-orange cap with wavy edge, false gills (ridges), fruity aroma
Cantharellus cibarius.survival_note: One of the most prized edible mushrooms. Check for false gills vs ridges to avoid toxic lookalike.

lookalikes.common_name: Devil's Bolete / ドクヤマドタケ
lookalikes.distinguishing: Boletus satanas has yellow/red coloration that turns blue when bruised

Boletus edulis.key_features: Brown to reddish-brown cap, white tubes that don't change color when bruised, bulbous stem
Boletus edulis.survival_note: Premium edible mushroom. Check color doesn't change to blue when cut.

Grifola frondosa.key_features: Large cluster of brown fan-shaped caps, grayish underside, growing at base of oak trees
Grifola frondosa.survival_note: Distinctive appearance makes it easy to identify. Excellent edible.

Oenanthe javanica.preparation_required: Cook thoroughly. Young shoots can be eaten raw with caution.

lookalikes.distinguishing: Cicuta has hollow chambered rhizome with septa; Oenanthe has solid rhizome and distinct aroma

Oenanthe javanica.key_features: Hollow stems, compound leaves with toothed edges, grows in shallow water
Oenanthe javanica.survival_note: Edible but has lethal lookalike (Water Hemlock). Cannot distinguish from photo alone. DO NOT EAT unless expert.

Allium victorialis.preparation_required: Can be eaten raw or cooked. Traditional Japanese mountain vegetable.

lookalikes.common_name: White Hellebore / バイケイソウ
lookalikes.distinguishing: Allium has onion/garlic odor when crushed; Veratrum has no smell

Allium victorialis.key_features: Broad leaves, onion-like bulb, distinct onion/garlic aroma when crushed
Allium victorialis.survival_note: Edible but has lethal lookalike (White Hellebore). Check for onion smell. If unsure, DO NOT EAT.

Artemisia indica.preparation_required: Young leaves can be eaten in tempura or added to rice. Older leaves bitter.
Artemisia indica.key_features: Silvery-green leaves (white underside), aromatic when crushed, yellow flowers in autumn
Artemisia indica.survival_note: Common wild edible. Low risk of misidentification. Distinctive aroma.
